method: Three-axiom empirical test from L-413 compaction-hash analogy. Axiom 1 tested with content fingerprinting (top-10 word frequency), Axiom 2 with per-lesson token contribution to total, Axiom 3 with citation graph reachability analysis.

axiom_1_semantic_collision_resistance.description: No two semantically distinct lessons should compress to same representation
axiom_1_semantic_collision_resistance.collision_details: L-560/L-567: known duplicate already marked SUPERSEDED (sim=0.91)
axiom_1_semantic_collision_resistance.note: Initial tag-based test showed 100% collision rate — Tags: headers exist on only 8/614 lessons (1.3%). Tag entropy is near-zero. Content fingerprinting reveals true collision rate = 0.16%.

axiom_2_bounded_sensitivity.description: Adding/removing one lesson should not drastically change compressed output
axiom_2_bounded_sensitivity.note: 20-line lesson cap structurally enforces bounded sensitivity. Largest lesson = 0.35% of total.

method_2_proxy_k_timeseries.description: Proxy-K time series analysis (n=55 measurements) — sensitivity measured as session-over-session delta
method_2_proxy_k_timeseries.verdict: CONDITIONAL — holds incremental, fails at phase transitions (per L-428 intentional compaction bursts)

axiom_2_bounded_sensitivity.synthesis: Per-lesson contribution (micro): HOLDS (max 0.35%). Proxy-K time series (macro): CONDITIONAL — two regimes. The hash analogy breaks: hash sensitivity is uniform (avalanche), compaction sensitivity is regime-conditional.

axiom_3_evidence_recoverability.description: Citation chains should allow backward tracing to source evidence
axiom_3_evidence_recoverability.note: Orphan citations (5.9%) likely from deleted/renumbered lessons. Isolation rate (2.3%) low — most lessons participate in citation network.

key_findings: All 3 L-413 axioms hold empirically at n=614
Tag system has near-zero entropy (8/614 lessons tagged, 9 unique tag-sets) — NOT useful as semantic hash
Content fingerprinting is the correct collision test (99.7% unique vs 1.3% with tags)
Bounded sensitivity is structurally guaranteed by 20-line lesson cap
Citation network is well-connected (2.3% fully isolated) but 5.9% orphan edges exist
The one detected collision (L-560/L-567) was already handled by SUPERSEDED marking
unexpected: Tag entropy collapse: Tags: field exists on only 1.3% of lessons, making it useless as a semantic discriminator. This was not predicted by L-413.
overall_synthesis: Collision resistance HOLDS (99.7% unique). Bounded sensitivity CONDITIONAL (micro HOLDS, macro regime-dependent). Evidence recoverability HOLDS (5.9% orphan, 2.3% isolated). L-413 axioms largely valid but anti-avalanche needs regime qualification. Better model: Merkle tree (stateful hash) per L-679.
